reasoning: The Lean statement negates the existence of a LinearOrder on ℂ such that ℂ becomes an IsStrictOrderedRing. Since ℂ already carries a Field/Semiring structure, combining it with LinearOrder and IsStrictOrderedRing yields exactly the classical notion of an ordered field: a total order compatible with addition, plus strict positivity of products of positives (via PosMulStrictMono and MulPosStrictMono). The let-binding properly brings the LinearOrder instance into scope so IsStrictOrderedRing can access the PartialOrder component. Therefore, asserting that no such (LinearOrder, IsStrictOrderedRing) pair exists on ℂ is logically equivalent to the informal claim that no order turns ℂ into an ordered field.
